questions.text: Kathputli is a famous puppet tradition associated with which state?
questions.explanation: Kathputli (from 'kath' = wood/cloth and 'putli' = doll) is the string puppet (marionette) tradition of Rajasthan, performed by the Nat community. The puppets have cloth bodies and no legs. They enact stories of Rajput heroes like Amar Singh Rathor.

questions.text: Koodiyattam, recognised by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage, is associated with which state?
questions.explanation: Koodiyattam is an ancient Sanskrit theatre tradition from Kerala, performed by the Chakyar community in temple stages (Koothambalams). It was the first Indian element added to UNESCO's ICH list (2001/2008). The Mizhavu is the special pot-drum used in Koodiyattam.

questions.text: Tholu Bommalata is a form of which type of puppetry?
questions.explanation: Tholu Bommalata ('Tholu' = leather, 'Bommalata' = puppet dance) is the shadow puppet tradition of Andhra Pradesh. The large translucent leather puppets are projected on a backlit white screen. They can be up to 5 feet tall and depict Ramayana stories.

questions.text: Ramlila — Traditional Performance of the Ramayana — was inscribed on UNESCO's ICH list in which year?
questions.explanation: Ramlila was inscribed on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list in 2008 (it was first recognised in 2005 under an earlier list format and formally integrated in 2008). Ramnagar's Ramlila near Varanasi is the most famous. It is a 30-day epic dramatic performance.

questions.text: Pavakathakali from Kerala is what type of puppet?
questions.explanation: Pavakathakali is a glove puppet (hand puppet) from Kerala that mimics Kathakali dance drama in miniature form with the same elaborate costumes and makeup as Kathakali. It is performed by a single puppeteer. Students often confuse it with string puppets because of the Kathakali connection.

questions.text: Putul Naach of West Bengal is which type of puppetry?
questions.explanation: Putul Naach (Bengali for 'puppet dance') of West Bengal refers to rod puppets — large puppets controlled by rods from below. These are distinct from string marionettes or shadow puppets. Jatra-style stories are performed with these rod puppets in rural Bengal.

questions.text: Ankiya Nat and Bhaona are theatrical traditions of Assam. Who created Ankiya Nat?
questions.explanation: Ankiya Nat (one-act plays) was created by Srimanta Sankardeva, the 15th-century Vaishnavite saint of Assam. These plays are performed in Sattra monastery courtyards. Bhaona is the dramatised public performance of Ankiya Nat scripts. Sankardeva also created Sattriya dance.

questions.text: Nautanki in UP is known by what alternative name in Haryana?
questions.explanation: Nautanki, the folk theatre form of Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan, is called Swang in Haryana and Punjab. Both feature melodramatic performances with songs and dialogues on historical and social themes. Tamasha is Maharashtra's form; Bidesia is Bihar's; Maach is Madhya Pradesh's.

questions.text: Teejan Bai is associated with which storytelling tradition of Chhattisgarh?
questions.explanation: Teejan Bai is the most famous exponent of Pandavani, a solo oral narration of the Mahabharata from Chhattisgarh. She received the Padma Vibhushan. Pandavani narrators perform with a single-string instrument (ektara) and dramatic gestures, enacting Mahabharata stories.

questions.text: Tolpavakoothu is a shadow puppet theatre tradition from Kerala performed in which type of setting?
questions.explanation: Tolpavakoothu (leather shadow puppet theatre) of Kerala is traditionally performed in temple compounds in specially built stages called Koothumadam. The leather puppets are projected on a long white cloth screen lit by oil lamps. It is dedicated to Goddess Bhagavati and enacts the Kamba Ramayana.

questions.text: Phad painting is a scroll tradition from Rajasthan. Which community performs with the Phad scroll?
questions.explanation: Phad painting depicts the stories of folk heroes Devnarayan and Pabuji on long cloth scrolls. Bhopa priests unroll and display the scroll at night by lamplight, singing the heroes' stories to the audience. This is a combined visual art + oral storytelling tradition unique to Rajasthan.

questions.text: Mudiyettu is a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage from Kerala that enacts the battle between which two figures?
questions.explanation: Mudiyettu is a ritual dance-drama from Kerala (inscribed in UNESCO ICH in 2010) that enacts the battle between Goddess Bhadrakali and the demon Darika. It is performed in fields or temples and has religious significance for the communities of central Kerala.

questions.text: Jatra is a popular folk theatre form associated primarily with which state?
questions.explanation: Jatra (also called Yatra) is the most popular folk theatre tradition of West Bengal, performed in open grounds with loud, dramatic singing, acting, and instrumental music. It also exists in Odisha as 'Odia Jatra'. Stories are based on mythology, history, and social issues.

questions.text: Ravanachhaya is a shadow puppet tradition from which state?
questions.explanation: Ravanachhaya ('Ravana's shadow') is a unique shadow puppet tradition from Odisha. The puppets are made of single-coloured deer skin with no joints — they are rigid (unlike the jointed, colourful Tholu Bommalata of Andhra Pradesh). Stories are from the Ramayana.

questions.text: Tamasha is a traditional folk theatre form from which state?
questions.explanation: Tamasha is a traditional folk theatre form from Maharashtra that integrates Lavani dance, comic dialogues, and social satire. Historically performed by the Kolhati community. Bal Gangadhar Tilak used public performances (Tamasha stages) to build mass consciousness.

questions.text: The oldest surviving Sanskrit theatre in the world is:
questions.explanation: Koodiyattam from Kerala is considered the world's oldest surviving Sanskrit theatre, with a continuous performance tradition of over 2,000 years. It is performed by the Chakyar community in temple stages. UNESCO recognised it as Intangible Cultural Heritage (2001/2008).
